title: International Terrestrial Reference System and Frame

infobox.value: 1997.0 | 4919 7909 8997 | First solution that combines unconstrained space geodesy solutions free from any [tectonic plate motion model](/w/index.php?title=Tectonic_plate_motion_model&action=edit&redlink=1 "Tectonic plate motion model (page does not exist)").[[4]](#cite_note-4) From this version onwards, the motion of the tectonic plate is represented in the solution for each station as a velocity vector. Previous ITRFs only continued the initial positions, using a motion model to fill in the velocity.

2000.0 | 4896 7910 8998 | Constructed with input data under the form of time series of station positions and Earth Orientation Parameters.[[5]](#cite_note-paper05-5) This version introduces extra parameters to describe the year-periodic motion of the stations: A (amplitude) and φ (phase) per-axis. This sort of seasonal variation has an amplitude of around 1 cm and is attributed to non-tidal loading effects (e.g. the shifting weight of water).
2005.0 | 5332 7911  8999 | Includes tropospheric modeling and improved solution methods.[[6]](#cite_note-ITRF2014-6)

2010.0 | 7789 7912 9000 | Generated with an enhanced modeling of nonlinear station motions.[[7]](#cite_note-7) Specifically:  * a semiannual component is added to the traditional annual periodic model; * smooth parametric fits are to model post-seismic deformation as opposed to the traditional approach using piecewise linear functions.

(**ITRS**) describes procedures for creating [reference frames](/wiki/Frame_of_reference "Frame of reference") suitable for use with measurements on or near the Earth's surface. This is done in much the same way that a [physical standard](/wiki/Standard_(metrology) "Standard (metrology)") might be described as a set of procedures for creating a *realization* of that standard. The ITRS defines a [geocentric](/wiki/Geocentric_coordinate_system "Geocentric coordinate system") system of coordinates using the [SI](/wiki/SI "SI") system of measurement.

(**ITRF**) is a realization of the ITRS. Its origin is at the center of mass of the whole earth including the oceans and atmosphere. New ITRF solutions are produced every few years, using the latest mathematical and surveying techniques to attempt to realize the ITRS as precisely as possible. Due to [experimental error](/wiki/Experimental_error "Experimental error"), any given ITRF will differ very slightly from any other realization of the ITRF. The difference between the latest as of 2006 [WGS 84](/wiki/World_Geodetic_System "World Geodetic System") (frame realisation G1150) and the latest ITRF2000 is only a few centimeters and RMS difference of one centimeter per component.[[1]](#cite_note-1) ITRFs are [Earth-centered, Earth-fixed (ECEF)](/wiki/Earth-centered,_Earth-fixed_coordinate_system "Earth-centered, Earth-fixed coordinate system") reference frames.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

World spatial reference system co-rotating with the Earth in its diurnal motion in space

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/90/ITRF-Fundamental-u.Satellitenstationen%2C46_po.jpg/250px-ITRF-Fundamental-u.Satellitenstationen%2C46_po.jpg)](/wiki/File:ITRF-Fundamental-u.Satellitenstationen,46_po.jpg)

ITRF reference stations

The **International Terrestrial Reference System** (**ITRS**) describes procedures for creating [reference frames](/wiki/Frame_of_reference "Frame of reference") suitable for use with measurements on or near the Earth's surface. This is done in much the same way that a [physical standard](/wiki/Standard_(metrology) "Standard (metrology)") might be described as a set of procedures for creating a *realization* of that standard. The ITRS defines a [geocentric](/wiki/Geocentric_coordinate_system "Geocentric coordinate system") system of coordinates using the [SI](/wiki/SI "SI") system of measurement.

An **International Terrestrial Reference Frame** (**ITRF**) is a realization of the ITRS. Its origin is at the center of mass of the whole earth including the oceans and atmosphere. New ITRF solutions are produced every few years, using the latest mathematical and surveying techniques to attempt to realize the ITRS as precisely as possible. Due to [experimental error](/wiki/Experimental_error "Experimental error"), any given ITRF will differ very slightly from any other realization of the ITRF. The difference between the latest as of 2006 [WGS 84](/wiki/World_Geodetic_System "World Geodetic System") (frame realisation G1150) and the latest ITRF2000 is only a few centimeters and RMS difference of one centimeter per component.[[1]](#cite_note-1) ITRFs are [Earth-centered, Earth-fixed (ECEF)](/wiki/Earth-centered,_Earth-fixed_coordinate_system "Earth-centered, Earth-fixed coordinate system") reference frames.

The ITRS and ITRF solutions are maintained by the [International Earth Rotation and Reference Systems Service](/wiki/International_Earth_Rotation_and_Reference_Systems_Service "International Earth Rotation and Reference Systems Service") ([IERS](/wiki/IERS "IERS")). Practical navigation systems are in general referenced to a specific ITRF solution, or to their own coordinate systems which are then referenced to an ITRF solution. For example, the *Galileo Terrestrial Reference Frame* (*GTRF*) is used for the [Galileo](/wiki/Galileo_(satellite_navigation) "Galileo (satellite navigation)") navigation system; currently defined as ITRF2005 by the [European Space Agency](/wiki/European_Space_Agency "European Space Agency").[[2]](#cite_note-gtrf-2)

## Versions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=International_Terrestrial_Reference_System_and_Frame&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Versions")]

The ITRF realizations developed from the ITRS since 1991 include the following versions:[[3]](#cite_note-QPS-3)

| Name | Ref. epoch | [EPSG](/wiki/EPSG "EPSG") code | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| ITRF91 | 1988.0 | 4913 7903 8991 |  |
| ITRF92 | 1988.0 | 4914 7904 8992 | First realization of the ITRS |
| ITRF93 | 1988.0 | 4915 7905 8993 |  |
| ITRF94 | 1993.0 | 4916 7906 8994 |  |
| ITRF96 | 1997.0 | 4917 7907 8995 |  |
| ITRF97 | 1997.0 | 4918 7908 8996 |  |
| ITRF2000 | 1997.0 | 4919 7909 8997 | First solution that combines unconstrained space geodesy solutions free from any [tectonic plate motion model](/w/index.php?title=Tectonic_plate_motion_model&action=edit&redlink=1 "Tectonic plate motion model (page does not exist)").[[4]](#cite_note-4) From this version onwards, the motion of the tectonic plate is represented in the solution for each station as a velocity vector. Previous ITRFs only continued the initial positions, using a motion model to fill in the velocity. |
| ITRF2005 | 2000.0 | 4896 7910 8998 | Constructed with input data under the form of time series of station positions and Earth Orientation Parameters.[[5]](#cite_note-paper05-5) This version introduces extra parameters to describe the year-periodic motion of the stations: A (amplitude) and φ (phase) per-axis. This sort of seasonal variation has an amplitude of around 1 cm and is attributed to non-tidal loading effects (e.g. the shifting weight of water). |
| ITRF2008 | 2005.0 | 5332 7911  8999 | Includes tropospheric modeling and improved solution methods.[[6]](#cite_note-ITRF2014-6) |
| ITRF2014 | 2010.0 | 7789 7912 9000 | Generated with an enhanced modeling of nonlinear station motions.[[7]](#cite_note-7) Specifically:  * a semiannual component is added to the traditional annual periodic model; * smooth parametric fits are to model post-seismic deformation as opposed to the traditional approach using piecewise linear functions. |
| ITRF2020 | 2015.0 | 9988 9989 9990 | [[8]](#cite_note-8) |

## Users

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=International_Terrestrial_Reference_System_and_Frame&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Users")]

GNSS systems:[[2]](#cite_note-gtrf-2)

* [Galileo](/wiki/Galileo_(satellite_navigation) "Galileo (satellite navigation)") Terrestrial Reference Frame (GTRF), ITRF2005; own implementation using IGS sites.
* [GPS](/wiki/GPS "GPS") just uses [WGS 84](/wiki/WGS_84 "WGS 84"), ITRF2020 since January 2024 (but used many versions of WGS 84 before), a little modified with International GNSS Service (IGS) implementation, IGS20.
* [BeiDou](/wiki/BeiDou "BeiDou") Coordinate System, China Terrestrial Reference Frame (CTRF) 2000 = ITRF97 at epoch 2000.0; own implementation.
* [GLONASS](/wiki/GLONASS "GLONASS") [PZ-90.11](/wiki/PZ-90 "PZ-90") is nominally its own system, but is quite close to ITRF and uses many of the same techniques.[[2]](#cite_note-gtrf-2)

National systems:

* United States: WGS 84 (see above); domestic use is mainly based on [NAD 83](/wiki/NAD_83 "NAD 83") instead.
* China: CTRF 2000 per above.

The GPS reference epoch was moved from 2000.0 to 2001.0 in G1150 due to the magnitude 7.9 Denali Fault earthquake in Alaska in November 2002. Still in 2022 ITRF2020 was released, yet GPS was only using G2139 in its antennas, which was aligned to ITRF2014 (IGb14) (though at epoch 2016.0, not reference epoch 2010.0).[[9]](#cite_note-NOAA2020-9) On 7 January 2024 move to IGS20 happened, so WGS 84 is now aligned with ITRF2020, including PSD (post-seismic deformation), also called G2296.

On the other hand GLONASS is using PZ-90.11, which is close to ITRF2008 at epoch 2011.0 and is using 2010.0 epoch (that means when you use reference transformation to PZ-90.11 you will get January 2010 date).
